channel.title: Business News - Markets reports and financial news from Sky

image.title: Business News - Markets reports and financial news from Sky

channel.description: Sky business news provides up to the minute reports on markets, share prices and the world economy, alongside expert business commentary.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Sky UK. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: British Steel taken into public ownership
item.description: British Steel has been taken into public ownership in the "national interest", the government has announced.
item.media:description: General view of the British Steel works in Scunthorpe. British Steel is set to return to public ownership as Sir Keir Starmer announced new laws he said will give the Government "options" to protect the industry and Scunthorpe workers. New powers could be used to nationalise British Steel, 38 years since the firm was first sold off. Picture date: Tuesday May 12, 2026. PA Photo. Photo credit should read: Joe Giddens/PA Wire

item.title: Economy flatlining as Reeves nears Number 11 exit
item.description: The UK economy grew by 0.1% in May, according to the final set of official figures ahead of Rachel Reeves's widely expected departure as chancellor.
item.media:description: Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rachel Reeves arrives to deliver a speech at the AI Adoption Summit, in the Smith Centre at the Science Museum,

item.title: Top Starmer business aide to stay on under Burnham | Mark Kleinman blog
TikTok under investigation in UK over child safety measures
item.description: Ofcom has launched an investigation into whether TikTok is failing to protect children from harmful content.

item.title: Parents face £1,100 bill for summer holiday childcare
item.description: Families face spending more than £1,100 per child for summer holiday childcare, new research suggests.
item.media:description: Medium low-angle shot of a mother and daughter playing with toys together on the floor in a community library in Newcastle-upon-Tyne, North

item.title: Average asking rent reaches record high
item.description: The average advertised private rent on a home in Britain has reached a record £1,397 per month, according to Rightmove.
item.media:description: London, UK-June 8, 2021: Real estate agent "To let " signs in front of terrace house in London.

item.title: Thames Water boss sees Burnham 'risk' as customer complaints surge 101%
item.description: Thames Water has warned funding is due to dry up by November and further money will be dependent on the views of incoming PM Andy Burnham, as its major lenders seek to shore up its financial future.
item.media:description: Thames Water vans are parked on a road as repair and maintenance work takes place, in London, Britain, April 3, 2024. REUTERS/Toby Melville

item.title: UK working to ban goods and services exports to and imports from Israeli settlements
item.description: The UK government is working on a ban on imports and exports to illegal Israeli settlements, the trade minister confirmed on Wednesday morning.
item.media:description: Palestinian students gather near a fence installed by Israeli settlers in their way to school, near Umm al-Khair village in Masafer Yatta, i

item.title: Firm that left 70,000 homes without water gets £30.5m penalty
item.description: Troubled South East Water (SEW) is facing a £30.5m redress package for multiple customer service failures, the industry watchdog has announced.
item.media:description: South East Water staff hand out bottled water at a water station in Maidstone on 13 January. Pic: PA

item.title: Next PM urged to pave way for bigger council tax rises
item.description: A cross-party group of MPs has told prime minister-in-waiting Andy Burnham he should allow councils to increase council tax by as much as they want.
item.media:description: Andy Burnham promised mass devolution as part of the "biggest rebalancing of power our country has seen" at a speech in Manchester in June.
